ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / NEW JERSEY

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

New Jersey Dental Assistant Registration

New Jersey Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in New Jersey run through New Jersey State Board of Dentistry. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 1 to 3 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the New Jersey State Board of Dentistry by registration.

  1. STEP 1

    Access the MyLicense Portal

    Navigate to the MyLicense Online Application System for the New Jersey State Board of Dentistry.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 3 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Create or Log In

    If you are a new user, create an account using the 'Register a New User' option. Existing users should log in with their credentials.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 3 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Initiate Application

    Select 'Board of Dentistry' and then 'Dental Assistant Radiologic Technologist (DRT)' from the application options to begin your online registration.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 3 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete Application & Upload Documents

    Fill out all required sections of the application form and upload necessary supporting documentation, such as proof of qualifications (e.g., DANB certifications, CODA-accredited program completion, CPR card).

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 3 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Fees

    Submit the required application fee ($100) and initial registration fee ($75) via the MyLicense portal to complete your submission.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 3 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Monitor Status

    Regularly check the MyLicense portal for updates on your application status and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Board.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 3 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$50
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$50

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Application page

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in New Jersey follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$75
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

Does New Jersey require a dental assistant license?

Yes, New Jersey requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

Is DANB certification recognized in New Jersey?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in New Jersey.

Can I take dental x-rays in New Jersey?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in New Jersey?

State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

Endorsement fee

$50

Processing time

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $75

New Jersey State Board of Dentistry
